batches.title: Soft-Boiled Eggs for Breakfast (Mon–Thu)
steps: Bring large pot of salted water to boil (enough to cover 14 eggs by 5 cm)
Gently lower 14 large eggs into boiling water using spoon
Set timer for 7 minutes (soft yolk, cooked white)
When timer sounds, immediately transfer eggs to ice bath (large bowl with ice water)
Let cool for at least 5 minutes until completely cool to touch
Gently crack and peel under cool running water, starting from blunt end
Store peeled eggs in airtight container in fridge, submerged in cold water; replace water daily
yields: 14 boiled eggs (7 eggs for each person Mon–Thu)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Breakfast — Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Berries: place 1 boiled egg in eggcup, toast bread with butter, add fresh raspberries
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Breakfast — Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Berries: place 1 boiled egg in eggcup, toast bread with butter, add fresh blueberries
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Breakfast — Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Berries: place 1 boiled egg in eggcup, toast bread with butter, add fresh strawberries
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Breakfast — Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Berries: place 1 boiled egg in eggcup, toast bread with butter, add fresh mixed berries

storage.safety_note: Hard-boiled eggs: 1 week in shell, 5 days peeled in water. Scrambled/cooked dishes: 3–4 days.

batches.title: Grilled Chicken Breasts & Roasted Mediterranean Vegetables
steps: Pat 800g chicken breasts dry with paper towels
Season generously on both sides with salt, pepper, dried oregano (1 tsp total), minced garlic (2 cloves), squeeze of lemon
Heat 1 tsp olive oil in large grill pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat until smoking
Grill chicken 6–7 min per side until internal temp reaches 75°C and juices run clear (no pink)
Transfer to cutting board, tent loosely with foil, rest 5 min
Meanwhile, cut 400g zucchini into 1 cm half-moons; cut 300g red bell peppers into 2 cm chunks; halve 200g cherry tomatoes
Toss all vegetables with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper, 1 tsp dried oregano, 2 minced garlic cloves
Spread on large baking sheet; roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden and tender
Cool chicken slightly, then slice into 1 cm strips; transfer to airtight container
Cool roasted vegetables to room temperature; transfer to separate airtight container (can store together as long as you use within 3 days)
Cook 200g dried quinoa per package directions (usually 1:2 ratio quinoa:water, simmer 15 min); drain and cool
Store cooked quinoa in airtight container
Refrigerate all three components separately until assembly

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mediterranean Grilled Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Quinoa: slice 180g grilled chicken, measure 150g roasted vegetables and 100g cooked quinoa, arrange on plate, drizzle with lemon & olive oil
day_of_assembly.Thursday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 4).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Shredded Ginger Chicken Filling
steps: Place 400g cooked chicken (from prep_batch_2, cooled) in medium bowl
Shred by hand or using two forks into 1–2 cm pieces
Mix 2 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp minced fresh ginger, 1 minced garlic clove in small bowl
Pour dressing over shredded chicken
Toss gently to coat evenly
Store in airtight container in fridge

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Asian Chicken Lettuce Cups with Bok Choy: reheat 150g shredded ginger chicken (30 sec in microwave or warm in pan), arrange 4 butter lettuce leaves on plate, spoon chicken into leaves, add 150g steamed bok choy, sprinkle sesame seeds

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Spiced Turkey Meatballs & Brown Lentil Salad
steps: Combine 500g ground turkey, 1 tsp dried oregano, 0.5 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp black pepper in large bowl
Mix gently with hands until just combined (do not overwork)
Shape into 18 equal meatballs (~25g each), place on parchment-lined baking sheet
Bake at 190°C for 18 minutes until internal temp reaches 75°C
Cool on baking sheet; transfer to airtight container, cover, refrigerate
Meanwhile, cook 200g dried brown lentils in 600 ml water with pinch of salt; simmer 25 min until tender but not mushy; drain if excess liquid remains, cool
Dice 100g cucumber into small pieces; halve 100g cherry tomatoes
Combine cooled lentils, cucumber, tomatoes, 2 tbsp fresh parsley, 1 tbsp fresh mint (minced) in bowl
Whisk together 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar, salt, pepper
Pour dressing over lentil salad, toss gently
Store in airtight container in fridge (lentils keep up to 5 days)

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch — Spiced Turkey Meatballs with Brown Lentil Salad: reheat 6 turkey meatballs in oven 180°C for 5 min, measure 180g lentil salad into bowl, arrange warm meatballs on top, serve
day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (red meat is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Spiced Ground Beef & Chickpea Base
steps: Heat 1 tsp olive oil in large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat
Add 400g lean ground beef (5% fat), breaking apart with wooden spoon
Cook 5–6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no pink remains; drain if needed
Add 1 tsp ground cumin, 0.5 tsp mild chilli powder, 1 tsp garlic powder, salt, pepper; stir to coat beef evenly
Cook 1 minute more until fragrant; remove from heat, cool slightly
Transfer to airtight container, cover, refrigerate
Open 1 can (400g) chickpeas, drain and rinse thoroughly in colander
Drain roasted red peppers from jar (use ~100g); chop into small dice
Combine chickpeas, red pepper dice, 1 tsp olive oil, juice of 0.5 lime, salt, pepper in bowl
Mix gently, transfer to airtight container, refrigerate

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner — Mexican-Style Ground Beef & Chickpea Bowl: warm 100g cooked ground beef in pan, add 80g chickpea mixture and warm through, arrange 150g romaine lettuce in bowl, top with warm beef mixture and 100g halved cherry tomatoes, squeeze fresh lime, sprinkle cilantro
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (red meat is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Chickpea & Vegetable Salad with Tahini Dressing
steps: Open 1 can (400g) chickpeas, drain and rinse thoroughly in colander until water runs clear
Drain roasted red peppers from jar; chop ~100g into 1 cm dice
Dice 100g cucumber (English or regular) into 1 cm cubes
Halve 100g cherry tomatoes
Chop 2 tbsp fresh parsley and 1 tbsp fresh mint leaves into fine ribbons
Combine chickpeas, red pepper, cucumber, tomatoes, parsley, mint in large bowl
Make tahini dressing: whisk 2 tbsp tahini, 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice, 1 tbsp water, 1 minced garlic clove, pinch of salt, pinch of black pepper
If dressing is too thick, add 1 tbsp more water a time until pourable but still creamy
Pour tahini dressing over vegetables, toss gently until all pieces are coated
Transfer to airtight container, cover, refrigerate (keeps up to 5 days)
yields: ~600g chickpea & vegetable salad with tahini dressing

day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (legumes is only safe ~5 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.

batches.title: Grilled Mediterranean Vegetables
steps: Rinse zucchini (200g), bell peppers (red/yellow, 150g), eggplant (100g); pat dry with paper towels
Slice zucchini lengthwise into 0.5 cm thick planks
Cut bell peppers into 2 cm thick strips, removing seeds and white membrane
Slice eggplant lengthwise into 0.5 cm thick planks (salt lightly, let sit 10 min to remove excess moisture, then pat dry)
Brush all vegetables lightly on both sides with 1 tsp olive oil total
Season generously with salt, black pepper, 0.5 tsp dried oregano
Heat grill pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat until just smoking
Grill zucchini 3 min per side until tender and golden; transfer to cutting board
Grill bell pepper 3 min per side; transfer to board
Grill eggplant 2 min per side until golden; transfer to board
Cool vegetables to room temperature
Chop all grilled vegetables into bite-sized (2–3 cm) pieces; store in airtight container in fridge (keeps 4 days)

day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (vegetables cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Cooked vegetables store well. Roasted veg can be eaten cold in salads.
